title: JSON Duality Views
description: Bidirectional document-relational mapping. Write through documents, read through SQL, and vice versa — with full ACID guarantees.
checkpoint: Same data. Two access patterns. One source of truth. No sync. No ETL. No eventual consistency.

exercises.title: Read Through the Document Lens
exercises.description: View clients, advisors, and transactions as rich JSON documents through duality views.
exercises.code:
-- View a client as a rich document (with nested accounts and holdings)
SELECT json_serialize(data PRETTY)
FROM client_portfolio_dv
WHERE data."_id" = 1001;

-- View an advisor's book
SELECT json_serialize(data PRETTY)
FROM advisor_book_dv
WHERE data."_id" = 5;

-- View a transaction with account context (unnested)
SELECT json_serialize(data PRETTY)
FROM transaction_feed_dv
WHERE data."_id" = 1;
exercises.validationQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM client_portfolio_dv WHERE data."_id" = 1001

exercises.title: Read Through the Relational Lens
exercises.description: Access the same data relationally with SQL JOINs — same data, different access pattern.
exercises.code:
-- The SAME data, accessed relationally
SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, c.risk_profile,
       a.account_type, a.account_name,
       h.symbol, h.quantity, h.market_value
FROM clients c
JOIN accounts a ON a.client_id = c.client_id
JOIN holdings h ON h.account_id = a.account_id
WHERE c.client_id = 1001
ORDER BY a.account_type, h.market_value DESC;
exercises.validationQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM clients c JOIN accounts a ON a.client_id = c.client_id JOIN holdings h ON h.account_id = a.account_id WHERE c.client_id = 1001

exercises.title: Write Through the Document API
exercises.description: Insert a new client with accounts and holdings through the duality view — one document insert populates three relational tables.
exercises.validationQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM clients WHERE email = 'sarah.chen@example.com'

exercises.title: Update Through the Document API
exercises.description: Update a holding's market value through the duality view and verify the relational table reflects the change.
exercises.validationQuery: SELECT h.market_value AS mv FROM holdings h JOIN accounts a ON h.account_id = a.account_id JOIN clients c ON a.client_id = c.client_id WHERE c.email = 'sarah.chen@example.com' AND h.symbol = 'NVDA'

exercises.title: Delete Through the Document API
exercises.description: Delete a client through the duality view and verify the cascade cleanup.
exercises.validationQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM clients WHERE email = 'sarah.chen@example.com'

exercises.title: Examine the Duality View Definition
exercises.description: Inspect the metadata that describes how the duality view maps between document and relational shapes.
exercises.validationQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM user_json_duality_view_tabs WHERE view_name = 'CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DV'

exercises.title: Create Your Own Duality View
exercises.description: Challenge: Create a holdings summary duality view that shows each holding with its account and client context using UNNEST.
exercises.validationQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM user_json_duality_views WHERE view_name = 'MY_HOLDINGS_DV'
